meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't scale projects (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't scale projects. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Starter Kit to Scale AI Projects
content.seo_description: Stop struggling to scale your student projects. Use this advanced SaaS starter kit checklist featuring Antigravity and Claude Code to build like a pro.
content.answer_block: To scale your SaaS, stop building boilerplate from scratch. Use a battle-tested SaaS starter kit to automate authentication, database schemas, and billing logic. By integrating Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for iterative implementation, you move from manual coding to high-level system orchestration, ensuring your project is production-ready from day one.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks crafting your first app, only to realize the architecture is a house of cards. Every new feature request feels like a monumental task, and the thought of scaling to actual users induces a cold sweat. This is the 'Student's Trap'—the silent killer of promising side projects where technical debt outpaces your progress.

But the industry's top 1% don't build this way. They leverage specialized starter kits that act as a force multiplier for their intent. By combining the structural rigor of Antigravity with the relentless velocity of Claude Code, you can bypass the tedious plumbing and focus entirely on the unique value proposition of your application.

steps.title: Define Modular Architecture
steps.description: Establish a clean separation of concerns to prevent monolith bloat. Use Antigravity to map your data models before writing a single line of code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a scalable, modular SaaS architecture schema using Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe, focusing on a multi-tenant database design.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize project structure based on the Antigravity architecture schema'

steps.title: Automate Authentication & RBAC
steps.description: Don't reinvent the security wheel. Implement robust Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) immediately to ensure your app is secure and ready for multi-user scaling.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure authentication middleware blueprint that integrates NextAuth with Supabase RLS policies for granular access control.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply authentication middleware and RLS policies to the existing codebase'

steps.title: Integrate Production-Grade Billing
steps.description: Connect Stripe webhooks early. Handling subscriptions, usage tracking, and invoice generation is non-negotiable for any scaling SaaS.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a Stripe webhook handler pattern that maps subscription events to database user status updates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement Stripe webhook listener and user subscription sync logic'

steps.title: Implement AI-Powered API Layers
steps.description: Wrap your AI features in a resilient API layer. This prevents direct coupling with LLM providers and allows for easy model swapping as your project grows.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Define a provider-agnostic AI API wrapper that handles streaming responses, rate limiting, and request logging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'build the AI API wrapper and integrate with the main backend controller'

steps.title: Deploy CI/CD Pipelines
steps.description: Automate your testing and deployment. A project that isn't automatically tested and deployed is a project that isn't truly scalable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Outline a CI/CD pipeline strategy for Vercel/GitHub Actions that includes automated linting, unit testing, and preview deployments.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure .github/workflows for automated testing and deployment'

pro_tips: Always build for multi-tenancy from day one, even if you only have one user; it forces cleaner data architecture.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests as you write the feature, not after; it creates a self-documenting feedback loop.
Decouple your business logic from your UI components to ensure your backend can be swapped or scaled independently.
Implement request logging early; you cannot fix bottlenecks if you don't have observability metrics from the start.
Treat your starter kit as a living codebase; update your dependencies weekly to avoid the dreaded 'framework drift'.
content.next_steps: After completing this checklist, focus on building your MVP's core feature, then immediately subject it to load testing to identify your next scaling bottleneck.
content.conversion_block: Your journey from student developer to SaaS architect starts with the tools you choose today. Stop fighting the scaling battle alone and start building with the professional infrastructure you deserve. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
